notes: Hi, thanks for attending the call. I tested the solution, it provide great insights but data refresh is too slow and feels clunky.
Fantastic support; everything works smoothly and the connectors are rock-solid.
Love the dashboards  but errors pop up when filtering huge datasets.

I want to thank all for attending the call and helping addressing the problem. However I am icluding here Manuel to express my disatissfaction with the team not solving this quickly and include the A team.

Hi Team, just a heads up — we noticed some tasks are failing on the PROD server due to XYZ server being unavailable. We've already flagged it with the infrastructure team and it looks like they are on top of it. Hopefully a quick fix! Thanks for your support as always.

Hi team,

 

with the release of Bitmarck 21c on March 21, 2026 new columns were added to 18 tables - initially -  as NULLABLE. These were subsequently changed to NOT NULL (presumably immediately after the columns were loaded during the release). These changes were not detected by Qlik and were not passed on to the target system. As a result, the new columns were created in the DWH as NULLABLE.

 

It was only through last Sunday’s reload (March 29, 2026) that Qlik correctly recreated the table structures. Subsequent processes in the DWH detected the structural differences and aborted. This is how this behavior was first noticed.

 

Qlik’s failure to make the structural adjustments to NOT NULL leads to the following problems:

 

1. Table structures are not passed on correctly

2. Aborted processes in the DWH due to structural differences

3. Additional manual effort in the DWH to correct the table structures

4. Without a reload, we would not have noticed the error at all!

 

We were able to reproduce the observed behavior on our test system.

 

We also tested the other way around – changing NOT NULL to NULL. This scenario is even worse as the replication process keeps running but in the end it results in a database error ‘cannot insert NULL’ on the target, which is written to the exception table (as was the case for the UPDATEs for missing rows).

 



 

Logged error:

 

RetCode: SQL_ERROR

SqlState: 23000

NativeError: 515

Message:

[Microsoft][ODBC Driver 18 for SQL Server][SQL Server]

Cannot insert the value NULL into column 'TestColumn', table 'CDC_LIVE_E.KKH_DATA_01.TestTable2';

column does not allow nulls. INSERT fails. Line: 1 Column: -1

 

From our perspective, this behavior should not occur, and we therefore request that a ticket be opened with technical support.
